items.blurb: A tiny hedgehog delivering a love letter, ready to stamp its way across every envelope and journal page you own.
A pensive little cat that says what you mean without a single word — press onto cards when someone is on your mind.
An excitable otter celebrating the day's post — the mascot every pen pal habit deserves.
Silver-foiled ribbons on clear film that catch the light every time you flip a planner page. Instant polish.
Little gems in shimmering silver foil — decorate a spread and watch it sparkle like a tiny treasure chest.
Five softly illustrated envelopes by Nishi Shuku that make even a grocery list feel like poetry in the post.
Featherlight washi writing paper with a fizzy cream soda motif — summer notes that practically float to the mailbox.
Die-cut washi letter paper with a raindrop texture and a cream soda theme — retro cafe daydreams, ready to mail.
Snoopy joins Zebra's beloved gel ink lineup — ten smooth colours to make your notes feel like a comic strip.
Dreamy translucent sticky notes by Imai Kira, all sugar-spun romance — too pretty to bury under a to-do list.
Imai Kira's romantic girly art on see-through sticky notes — leave reminders that look like love letters to yourself.
A slice of Subikiawa charm on translucent paper — sticky notes that turn any margin into a patisserie window.
Forty die-cut stickers from the little bird shopping street's cosiest corner bar — tiny nightlife for your journal.
Little birds catch a matinee in this 40-piece flake sticker pack — retro cinema charm for planners and letters.
The bird town boutique opens its doors: 40 die-cut stickers of tiny shopfront fashion for your pages.
Dinner service on the little bird shopping street — 40 flake stickers of feathered fine dining.
Sticky notes straight from the bird town bar — jot reminders with a side of retro shopping-street whimsy.
Notes from the bird town cinema — retro movie-house sticky notes for plans worth a standing ovation.
The bird town restaurant takes your order — sticky notes with retro shotengai charm for desk and planner.
Rub-on gold foil travel motifs that transfer like magic — gilded wanderlust for journal spreads.
Elegant hand motifs in rub-on gold foil — a graceful gilded touch for minimalist pages.
Golden blooms you rub straight onto the page — instant flower-press glamour, no glue required.
A garden in gold leaf: rub-on floral transfers that make every journal entry feel gilded.
Botanical sketches in shimmering rub-on gold — herbarium elegance for planners and notebooks.
Twenty sheets of translucent cloud-print paper for collage, layering and daydream-grade gift wrap.
Sheer fruity-print tracing paper — twenty sheets of juicy translucence for collage and tiny wraps.
Lilac-toned translucent papers in five spring patterns — layer them and watch your journal bloom.
Holographic KITTA strips in a wallet-sized folder — pre-cut sparkle that lives in your planner pocket.
Clear die-cut patch-style stickers tucked in a pocketable folder — peel, place, and prettify on the go.
Keina Higashide's dreamy watercolour drips as clear film stickers — a melty little folder of art for your pages.
A Chamil Garden rubber stamp with quiet, painterly charm — a limited pressing for journal keepers who collect secrets.
Another whisper from Chamil Garden: a limited-run rubber stamp with soft artistic detail for planner pages.
Soft-toned rub-on transfers that sink right into the page — cosy illustrated warmth with zero sticker edges.
Warm-hued florals you rub onto paper like a secret garden — seamless, sticker-free decoration.
The bird town sweets shop in 40 die-cut stickers — pastries, birds, and pure retro-street delight.
A stand-up sticky note from the bird town sweets shop — dessert-counter cuteness that perches on your desk.
Thirty mini washi sheets topped with melon soda and a cherry — bite-sized notes with a retro cafe fizz.
Writing paper die-cut into a frosty orange cream soda — a note that arrives like dessert.
Postage-stamp nostalgia on soft mino washi — a summer letter set for committed snail-mailers.
Pineapples and flamingos on delicate washi — tropical letters that feel like a vacation in an envelope.
Die-cut stamp-themed washi with a raindrop texture — for letters that celebrate the art of sending them.
Rain-textured washi cut into tropical summer shapes — pineapple-and-flamingo mail with serious charm.
Melon soda with a cherry on top, shrunk to mini letter size — 30 washi sheets of fizzy nostalgia.
A mini washi letter set starring melon cream soda — small sheets, big retro cafe energy.
Yohaku's collage-style washi tape, torn-paper texture and quiet shimmer — understated beauty by the metre.
Candy-bright washi tape with hot-stamped sparkle — a sweet glimmer for wrapping and journaling.
Fifty-four flower flake stickers by Kurogoma — a pocket meadow to scatter across planners and letters.
Sunny Sunday's celebration-themed washi with gold hot-stamp accents — confetti energy on a roll.
Fifty-four perfume-bottle stickers by Waka Nakauchi — vanity-table elegance for your journal spreads.
Oversized retro Japan amusement park stickers — two sheets of ferris-wheel nostalgia for big journal moments.
Two sheets of large retro kissaten stickers — vintage Japanese cafe culture for maximalist spreads.
Three months of clean date numbers to decorate your own way — the minimalist backbone of a pretty planner.
Petite date-number stickers for tidy planner spreads — simple, flexible, endlessly restyleable.
A tiny cat curled up in a shoe, pin-ready for bags and jackets — irresistible to any cat person you know.
Teeny colourful flower stickers made for cramming joy into every planner corner.
Miniature jewellery stickers with maximum sparkle-per-millimetre — a treasure box for your pages.
Five bright-but-gentle Mildliner brush pens, dual-tipped and smudge-shy — planner highlighting at its dreamiest.
The Shitashimi set of Mildliner brush pens — five friendly muted shades that flatter every notebook page.
Gem stickers hot-stamped for a light-catching gleam — a jewellery box that fits in your planner.
Handmade organic washi from a mountain-village studio in Gifu — rare, textural paper for slow crafting.
Keina Higashide's watercolour shapes on clear tape — decorate paper, glass, or jars with translucent art.
Gift-motif clear tape that dresses up wrapping, jars and journal pages with see-through charm.
An artist's atelier in clear photopolymer stamps — ink your own tiny studio scenes across cards and journals.
Boutique shopfront motifs as clear stamps — stamp a stylish little high street wherever you please.
Retro Japanese cafe scenes in clear stamp form — pour a little kissaten nostalgia onto every page.
A wandering cat goes chestnut picking in this autumn postcard by Toshinori Mori — seasonal Japan, feline-guided.
Yohaku's Point washi tape layers old-paper fragments into quiet collage — subtle texture for thoughtful spreads.
The Design roll from Yohaku — soft, scrap-paper elegance that makes any collage look intentional.
Clear stamps celebrating your favourite people — ready-made warmth for cards, gifts and scrapbook pages.
Travel-themed clear stamps for documenting adventures — your trip journal's new best friend.
Classical roses as rub-on transfers — vintage romance that melts seamlessly into the page.
Miki Tamura's torn-washi collage art, winter edition — layered light and shadow in sticker form.
A little town built from torn washi tape — Miki Tamura's collage stickers bring quiet streets to your pages.
Forest scenes in layered washi shadow and light — Miki Tamura stickers for woodland-hearted journalers.
Botanical collage stickers pieced from tiny washi fragments — Miki Tamura's garden, page-ready.
Kitchen scenes in torn-washi collage — homely warmth from Miki Tamura, one sticker at a time.
Tea and sweets rendered in layered washi tape — Miki Tamura's cosiest sticker sheet yet.
Spools, scissors and stitches in washi collage — a love letter to craft corners everywhere.
A travelling cat pauses by golden rice fields — Toshinori Mori's autumn postcard for wanderers and cat lovers.
Kurogoma's petit flowers marching along a washi roll — playful blooms for seven metres of crafting.
